question_text: Which statement best describes the law of conservation of energy?

options.label: A
options.text: Energy can be created and destroyed easily

options.label: B
options.text: Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transferred or transformed

options.label: C
options.text: Energy is only found in moving objects

options.label: D
options.text: Energy always stays in the same form

explanation: The law of conservation of energy is a fundamental physics principle stating that energy cannot be created or destroyed; it can only be transferred between objects or transformed from one form to another. This means the total amount of energy in a closed system always stays the same.
